findings.description: Nested <main> landmark elements - ensure the document has at most one main landmark at top level
findings.evidence: <main> at line 27 contains another <main> at line 33. Only one <main> landmark is valid per document.

findings.description: The document title and subtitle headings use h1 and h2 in a reasonable way, but the two h2 elements ('Brownfields Property Name' and 'Brownfields Project Number') could be considered sub-headings of the title rather than separate sections. However, this is a reasonable structural choice and only a minor observation.
findings.evidence: PDF has a centered block title 'SUMMARY OF NOTICE OF INTENT TO REDEVELOP A BROWNFIELDS PROPERTY' with subtitles 'Brownfields Property Name: Colwick Road Property' and 'Brownfields Project Number: 25095-21-060'. HTML uses h1 for the main title and h2 for the two subtitles.

findings.description: The HTML has nested <main> elements — an outer <main> wrapping everything and an inner <main> for the body content. This is an HTML structural issue but does not cause content duplication or data loss.
findings.evidence: HTML source contains '<main>' at the top level and another '<main>' inside after the '<header>' block.
